tasks.title: Create DestinationType Zod enum and DESTINATION_TYPE_LEVELS constant
tasks.description:
Create the DestinationType Zod enum in `packages/schemas/src/enums/destination-type.enum.ts` and its corresponding schema file `packages/schemas/src/enums/destination-type.schema.ts`. Define the enum values: COUNTRY, REGION, PROVINCE, DEPARTMENT, CITY, TOWN, NEIGHBORHOOD. Create the `DESTINATION_TYPE_LEVELS` constant mapping each type to its numeric level (0-6). Export both from `packages/schemas/src/enums/index.ts`.

**Files to create/modify:**
- `packages/schemas/src/enums/destination-type.enum.ts` (new)
- `packages/schemas/src/enums/destination-type.schema.ts` (new)
- `packages/schemas/src/enums/index.ts` (add exports)

**Test requirements:**
- Unit test: `packages/schemas/test/enums/destination-type.test.ts`
- Test all enum values are valid
- Test DESTINATION_TYPE_LEVELS maps correctly
- Test Zod schema validates valid values and rejects invalid ones

**Acceptance criteria:**
- DestinationTypeEnum has 7 values matching spec
- DESTINATION_TYPE_LEVELS maps COUNTRY=0, REGION=1, ..., NEIGHBORHOOD=6
- Follows existing enum pattern (see role.enum.ts, visibility.enum.ts for reference)

tasks.title: Add hierarchy columns to destinations table
tasks.description:
Add 5 new columns to the `destinations` table in `packages/db/src/schemas/destination/destination.dbschema.ts`:
1. `parentDestinationId` (UUID, self-referencing FK, ON DELETE RESTRICT)
2. `destinationType` (DestinationTypePgEnum, NOT NULL)
3. `level` (integer, NOT NULL, default 0)
4. `path` (text, NOT NULL, UNIQUE) - materialized path e.g. '/argentina/entre-rios/concepcion-del-uruguay'
5. `pathIds` (text, NOT NULL) - ancestor IDs e.g. 'uuid1/uuid2/uuid3'

**Files to modify:**
- `packages/db/src/schemas/destination/destination.dbschema.ts` (add columns)

**Test requirements:**
- TypeScript compilation check
- Verify column types and constraints match spec

**Acceptance criteria:**
- All 5 columns added with correct types
- parentDestinationId references destinations.id with ON DELETE RESTRICT
- path has UNIQUE constraint
- destinationType uses DestinationTypePgEnum
- level defaults to 0

tasks.title: Add findDescendants method to DestinationModel
tasks.description:
Add the `findDescendants` method to `packages/db/src/models/destination/destination.model.ts`. This method uses the materialized path (pathIds) to find all descendants of a given destination.

**Method signature:**
```ts
public async findDescendants(
    destinationId: string,
    options?: { maxDepth?: number; destinationType?: DestinationType }
): Promise<Destination[]>
```

**Implementation:**
1. Find parent destination by ID
2. Query WHERE pathIds LIKE parent.pathIds + '/' + parent.id + '%'
3. Filter by deletedAt IS NULL
4. Apply maxDepth filter (level <= parent.level + maxDepth)
5. Apply destinationType filter if provided
6. Return results ordered by level ASC

**Files to modify:**
- `packages/db/src/models/destination/destination.model.ts`

**Test requirements:**
- Unit test: Returns all descendants
- Unit test: Respects maxDepth filter
- Unit test: Filters by destinationType
- Unit test: Returns empty for leaf nodes
- Unit test: Excludes soft-deleted descendants

**Acceptance criteria:**
- SQL uses LIKE on pathIds for efficient querying
- Respects soft delete filter
- maxDepth and destinationType filters work correctly

subtasks.title: Implement findDescendants with pathIds LIKE query

tasks.title: Add findAncestors method to DestinationModel
tasks.description:
Add the `findAncestors` method to `packages/db/src/models/destination/destination.model.ts`. This method uses pathIds to efficiently fetch all ancestors.

**Method signature:**
```ts
public async findAncestors(destinationId: string): Promise<Destination[]>
```

**Implementation:**
1. Find destination by ID
2. Split pathIds by '/' to get ancestor IDs
3. Fetch all ancestors in single query using WHERE id = ANY(ancestorIds)
4. Filter by deletedAt IS NULL
5. Order by level ASC (root first)

**Files to modify:**
- `packages/db/src/models/destination/destination.model.ts`

**Test requirements:**
- Unit test: Returns ancestors in correct order (root to parent)
- Unit test: Returns empty array for top-level destination (country)
- Unit test: Handles empty pathIds
- Unit test: Excludes soft-deleted ancestors

**Acceptance criteria:**
- Single query fetches all ancestors (no N+1)
- Results ordered root-to-parent (level ASC)
- Empty array for root-level destinations

subtasks.title: Single query with WHERE id = ANY

tasks.title: Add isDescendant and updateDescendantPaths methods to DestinationModel
tasks.description:
Add two utility methods to `packages/db/src/models/destination/destination.model.ts`.

**isDescendant method:**
```ts
public async isDescendant(potentialDescendantId: string, ancestorId: string): Promise<boolean>
```
Checks if potentialDescendantId's pathIds contains ancestorId. Used for cycle detection.

**updateDescendantPaths method:**
```ts
public async updateDescendantPaths(parentId: string, oldPath: string, newPath: string): Promise<void>
```
Finds all descendants and replaces oldPath prefix with newPath in each descendant's path. Used during reparenting.

**Files to modify:**
- `packages/db/src/models/destination/destination.model.ts`

**Test requirements:**
- Unit test: isDescendant returns true for actual descendants
- Unit test: isDescendant returns false for non-descendants
- Unit test: isDescendant returns false for self
- Unit test: updateDescendantPaths updates all paths correctly
- Unit test: updateDescendantPaths handles nested descendants

**Acceptance criteria:**
- isDescendant is O(1) lookup using pathIds.includes()
- updateDescendantPaths correctly replaces path prefixes
- No orphaned paths after update

subtasks.title: Implement updateDescendantPaths using findDescendants + batch update

tasks.title: Update _beforeCreate hook for hierarchy auto-computation
tasks.description:
Override `_beforeCreate` in `packages/service-core/src/services/destination/destination.service.ts` to auto-compute hierarchy fields when creating a destination.

**Logic:**
1. If `parentDestinationId` provided:
   - Fetch parent destination
   - Validate parent exists
   - Validate destinationType matches expected level (parent.level + 1)
   - Compute level = parent.level + 1
   - Compute path = parent.path + '/' + slug
   - Compute pathIds = parent.pathIds + '/' + parent.id (or parent.pathIds/parent.id)
2. If no parent (top-level):
   - Validate destinationType is COUNTRY
   - Set level = 0
   - Set path = '/' + slug
   - Set pathIds = ''

**Files to modify:**
- `packages/service-core/src/services/destination/destination.service.ts`

**Test requirements:**
- See T-026 for comprehensive tests

**Acceptance criteria:**
- Path auto-computed correctly for all hierarchy levels
- pathIds chain built correctly
- Level matches parent.level + 1
- COUNTRY validation for parentless destinations
- ServiceError thrown for invalid parent/type combinations

subtasks.title: Add parent lookup and validation in _beforeCreate
Compute path, pathIds, level from parent

tasks.title: Update _beforeUpdate hook for reparenting logic
tasks.description:
Override `_beforeUpdate` in `packages/service-core/src/services/destination/destination.service.ts` to handle reparenting (changing parentDestinationId) and slug changes.

**Logic:**
1. If `parentDestinationId` or `slug` changed:
   - Fetch current destination
   - Determine new parent ID and slug
   - **Cycle detection**: If new parent, check isDescendant(newParentId, currentId)
   - Validate parent exists and type/level are compatible
   - Compute new path, pathIds, level
   - If path changed: call updateDescendantPaths(id, oldPath, newPath) to cascade
2. If neither changed, return empty (no hierarchy updates)

**Files to modify:**
- `packages/service-core/src/services/destination/destination.service.ts`

**Test requirements:**
- See T-026 for comprehensive tests

**Acceptance criteria:**
- Cycle detection prevents setting descendant as parent
- Path recalculated on parent change
- All descendants updated recursively on path change
- Slug change updates path for self and all descendants
- ServiceError for cycle detection with clear message

subtasks.title: Handle edge cases (same parent, null parent)

tasks.description:

**Method signature:**
```ts
public async getChildren(actor: Actor, params: GetDestinationChildrenInput): Promise<ServiceOutput<{ children: Destination[] }>>
```

**Implementation:**
1. Use `runWithLoggingAndValidation` wrapper
2. Validate input with `GetDestinationChildrenInputSchema`
3. Find parent destination, check it exists
4. Check permission: `checkCanViewDestination(actor, parent)`
5. Query model.findAll({ parentDestinationId: id, deletedAt: null })
6. Return { children: items }

**Files to modify:**
- `packages/service-core/src/services/destination/destination.service.ts`

**Test requirements:**
- See T-025 for comprehensive tests

**Acceptance criteria (US-008):**
- Returns only immediate children (not grandchildren)
- Returns empty array for leaf destinations
- Permission check enforced
- 404 for non-existent parent ID

tasks.title: Create hierarchy node seed files
tasks.description:
Create 4 new seed JSON files for hierarchy nodes in `packages/seed/src/data/destination/hierarchy/`.

**Files to create:**
1. `000-country-argentina.json` - Country node (level 0)
2. `001-region-litoral.json` - Region node (level 1, parent: argentina)
3. `002-province-entre-rios.json` - Province node (level 2, parent: litoral)
4. `003-department-uruguay.json` - Department node (level 3, parent: entre-rios)

**Each file must include:**
- Standard destination fields (id, slug, name, summary, description)
- Hierarchy fields (destinationType, level, path, pathIds, parentDestinationId)
- Location, media, SEO metadata
- Proper pathIds chains reflecting parent hierarchy
- moderationState: APPROVED, visibility: PUBLIC, lifecycleState: ACTIVE

**Follow spec section 5.1 for exact data.**

**Files to create:**
- `packages/seed/src/data/destination/hierarchy/` directory (new)
- 4 JSON files as described above

**Acceptance criteria:**
- All 4 files follow existing destination seed format
- Hierarchy fields are consistent (path matches slug chain, level matches type)
- parentDestinationId references correct parent seed ID

tasks.title: Update existing 11 city seed files with hierarchy fields
tasks.description:
Update all 11 existing destination seed files to include hierarchy fields. All cities belong under the 'Departamento Uruguay' hierarchy node.

**Files to modify (all in `packages/seed/src/data/destination/`):**
- 001-destination-chajari.json
- 002-destination-colon.json
- 003-destination-concordia.json
- 004-destination-federacion.json
- 005-destination-gualeguaychu.json
- 006-destination-ibicuy.json
- 007-destination-liebig.json
- 008-destination-paranacito.json
- 009-destination-san-jose.json
- 010-destination-ubajay.json
- 011-destination-concepcion-del-uruguay.json

**Fields to add to each:**
- `destinationType`: "CITY"
- `level`: 4
- `path`: "/argentina/litoral/entre-rios/depto-uruguay/{slug}"
- `pathIds`: "/000-country-argentina/001-region-litoral/002-province-entre-rios/003-department-uruguay"
- `parentDestinationId`: "003-department-uruguay"

**Note:** Some cities may belong to different departments. For now, assign all to Depto. Uruguay per the spec. Future specs may refine this.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- All 11 files updated with consistent hierarchy fields
- Paths match slug pattern
- pathIds reference correct hierarchy chain

subtasks.title: Add hierarchy fields to Chajari, Colon, Concordia, Federacion

tasks.title: Update seed script and manifest for hierarchy order
tasks.description:
Update the seed script and manifest to process hierarchy nodes BEFORE cities, respecting parent-child insertion order.

**Changes needed:**
1. Update `packages/seed/src/manifest-required.json`:
   - Add hierarchy files before city files
   - Order: argentina -> litoral -> entre-rios -> depto-uruguay -> cities
2. Update `packages/seed/src/required/destinations.seed.ts`:
   - Handle hierarchy fields in normalizer (exclude pathIds, path, level from normalizer exclusion if service computes them, or include them if seed data provides them directly)
   - Ensure parentDestinationId is mapped through idMapper
3. Verify seed script handles the self-referencing FK correctly

**Files to modify:**
- `packages/seed/src/manifest-required.json`
- `packages/seed/src/required/destinations.seed.ts`

**Test requirements:**
- Run `pnpm db:fresh` and verify all destinations seeded correctly
- Verify hierarchy relationships are correct in DB

**Acceptance criteria:**
- Seed runs without FK constraint errors
- Hierarchy nodes created before cities
- parentDestinationId correctly mapped via idMapper
- All 15 destinations (4 hierarchy + 11 cities) seeded

subtasks.title: Update seed normalizer for hierarchy fields

tasks.title: Create GET /destinations/:id/children API route
tasks.description:
Create a new API route for fetching immediate children of a destination.

**Endpoint:** `GET /api/v1/public/destinations/{id}/children`

**Implementation:**
- Use `createCRUDRoute` factory
- Request params: { id: DestinationIdSchema }
- Response schema: array of DestinationListItemSchema
- Handler: call `destinationService.getChildren(actor, { destinationId: id })`
- Public route (skipAuth: true)

**Files to create:**
- `apps/api/src/routes/destination/children.ts` (new)

**Test requirements:**
- See T-037 for integration tests

**Acceptance criteria (US-008):**
- Returns 200 with array of children
- Returns 200 with empty array for leaf destination
- Returns 404 for non-existent parent ID
- Response matches DestinationListItemSchema

tasks.title: Create GET /destinations/:id/descendants API route
tasks.description:
Create a new API route for fetching all descendants of a destination.

**Endpoint:** `GET /api/v1/public/destinations/{id}/descendants`

**Implementation:**
- Use `createCRUDRoute` factory
- Request params: { id: DestinationIdSchema }
- Request query: { maxDepth?: number, destinationType?: DestinationType }
- Response schema: array of DestinationListItemSchema
- Handler: call `destinationService.getDescendants(actor, { destinationId, maxDepth, destinationType })`
- Public route (skipAuth: true)

**Files to create:**
- `apps/api/src/routes/destination/descendants.ts` (new)

**Test requirements:**
- See T-037 for integration tests

**Acceptance criteria (US-009):**
- Returns all descendants in flat array
- Respects maxDepth and destinationType query params
- Returns 200 with empty array for leaf destination
- Returns 404 for non-existent parent ID

subtasks.title: Parse maxDepth and destinationType from query

tasks.title: Create GET /destinations/:id/ancestors API route
tasks.description:
Create a new API route for fetching all ancestors of a destination.

**Endpoint:** `GET /api/v1/public/destinations/{id}/ancestors`

**Implementation:**
- Use `createCRUDRoute` factory
- Request params: { id: DestinationIdSchema }
- Response schema: array of destinations (id, slug, name, level, destinationType, path)
- Handler: call `destinationService.getAncestors(actor, { destinationId: id })`
- Public route (skipAuth: true)

**Files to create:**
- `apps/api/src/routes/destination/ancestors.ts` (new)

**Test requirements:**
- See T-037 for integration tests

**Acceptance criteria (US-010):**
- Returns ancestors ordered root-to-parent
- Returns 200 with empty array for country
- Returns 404 for non-existent destination

tasks.title: Create GET /destinations/:id/breadcrumb API route
tasks.description:
Create a new API route for fetching breadcrumb trail of a destination.

**Endpoint:** `GET /api/v1/public/destinations/{id}/breadcrumb`

**Implementation:**
- Use `createCRUDRoute` factory
- Request params: { id: DestinationIdSchema }
- Response schema: array of BreadcrumbItemSchema
- Handler: call `destinationService.getBreadcrumb(actor, { destinationId: id })`
- Public route (skipAuth: true)
- cacheTTL: 3600 (breadcrumbs rarely change)

**Files to create:**
- `apps/api/src/routes/destination/breadcrumb.ts` (new)

**Test requirements:**
- See T-037 for integration tests

**Acceptance criteria (US-004, US-010):**
- Returns breadcrumb including ancestors + current destination
- Each item matches BreadcrumbItemSchema
- Returns only current destination for country
- Returns 404 for non-existent destination

tasks.title: Create GET /destinations/by-path API route
tasks.description:
Create a new API route for resolving a destination by its materialized path.

**Endpoint:** `GET /api/v1/public/destinations/by-path`

**Implementation:**
- Use `createCRUDRoute` factory
- Request query: { path: string }
- Response schema: DestinationSchema
- Handler: call `destinationService.getByPath(actor, { path })`
- Public route (skipAuth: true)
- cacheTTL: 300

**IMPORTANT:** This route must be registered BEFORE the `:id` route to avoid path conflicts. The path '/by-path' would be matched as an ID otherwise.

**Files to create:**
- `apps/api/src/routes/destination/byPath.ts` (new)

**Test requirements:**
- See T-037 for integration tests

**Acceptance criteria (US-011):**
- Resolves destination by exact path match
- Returns 404 for non-existent path
- Uses path index for performance
- Query param `path` is required

tasks.title: Update GET /destinations (list/search) with hierarchy filters
tasks.description:
Update the existing destination list route to accept and pass hierarchy filter parameters.

**Files to modify:**
- `apps/api/src/routes/destination/list.ts`

**Changes:**
1. Add hierarchy query params to route definition (requestQuery)
2. Extract hierarchy params from query: parentDestinationId, destinationType, level, ancestorId
3. Pass hierarchy params to service.list() or service.search()

**Test requirements:**
- See T-037 for integration tests

**Acceptance criteria (US-001, US-002, US-007):**
- `GET /destinations?destinationType=CITY` returns only cities
- `GET /destinations?level=2` returns only provinces
- `GET /destinations?parentDestinationId=X` returns direct children
- `GET /destinations?ancestorId=X` returns all descendants
- Existing pagination and sorting still work
- Backward compatible (no filters = existing behavior)

tasks.title: Register new hierarchy routes in destination router

**Routes to register (in correct order):**
1. `GET /by-path` (BEFORE /:id routes to avoid path conflicts)
2. `GET /:id/children`
3. `GET /:id/descendants`
4. `GET /:id/ancestors`
5. `GET /:id/breadcrumb`

**IMPORTANT:** The `/by-path` route must be registered before routes that match `/:id` to prevent 'by-path' being interpreted as an ID.

**Test requirements:**

- No conflicts with existing routes

**Acceptance criteria:**
- All 5 new routes registered and accessible
- No route conflicts
- Route ordering prevents path/ID ambiguity

tasks.title: Write integration tests for hierarchy API routes
tasks.description:
Write comprehensive integration tests for all hierarchy API endpoints.

- `apps/api/test/routes/destination/hierarchy.test.ts` (new)

**Test suites:**
1. GET /destinations/:id/children:

2. GET /destinations/:id/descendants:

**Acceptance criteria:**

- Response shapes validated against schemas

tasks.title: Update getDestinationUrl utility for hierarchical URLs
tasks.description:
Update the URL generation utility in the web app to support hierarchical destination URLs using the `path` field.

**Files to modify:**
- `apps/web/src/utils/urls.ts` (or create if not exists)

**Function to create/update:**
```ts
export function getDestinationUrl(params: { destination?: { path: string }; path?: string }): string
```
- Takes destination object or path string
- Returns `/destinos${path}` (e.g., `/destinos/argentina/entre-rios/concepcion-del-uruguay`)
- Throws if no path provided

**Update all existing references** to destination URL generation to use the new path-based function.

**Test requirements:**
- Unit test: Correct URL for various hierarchy depths
- Unit test: Error for missing path

**Acceptance criteria (US-003):**
- URLs reflect geographic hierarchy
- Old slug-based URLs can redirect to new hierarchical URLs (if applicable)
- Function is reusable across all components

subtasks.title: Find and update all existing destination URL references

tasks.title: Create Breadcrumb Astro component
tasks.description:
Create or update the Breadcrumb component to display hierarchical destination navigation.

**Files to create/modify:**
- `apps/web/src/components/shared/Breadcrumb.astro` (new or update)

**Component props:**
- `breadcrumb: BreadcrumbItem[]` from @repo/schemas

**Rendering:**
1. `<nav aria-label="Breadcrumb">` wrapper
2. `<ol>` with list items for each breadcrumb segment
3. First items: 'Inicio' (/) and 'Destinos' (/destinos)
4. Each ancestor: link to `/destinos{item.path}`
5. Last item: `<span aria-current="page">` (no link)
6. Separator character '>' between items

**Styling:** Use existing design system (Tailwind classes per project conventions)

**Accessibility:**
- `aria-label="Breadcrumb"` on nav
- `aria-current="page"` on current item
- Keyboard navigable links

**Test requirements:**
- Visual verification
- Accessibility check (semantic HTML, ARIA)

**Acceptance criteria (US-004):**
- Displays full hierarchy path
- Each segment clickable (except current)
- Accessible markup
- Responsive (wraps on mobile)

subtasks.title: Add Inicio and Destinos base items
Render ancestor links from breadcrumb prop

tasks.title: Create dynamic destination route [...path].astro
tasks.description:
Create a catch-all Astro route that handles hierarchical destination URLs.

**Files to create:**
- `apps/web/src/pages/destinos/[...path].astro` (new)

**Implementation:**
1. Extract `path` from Astro.params
2. Build full path: `/${path}` (ensure leading slash)
3. Call API to fetch destination by path (getByPath)
4. If not found, redirect to 404
5. Call API to fetch breadcrumb for destination
6. Call API to fetch children of destination
7. Render:
   - Breadcrumb component at top
   - Destination details (name, summary, description)
   - If children exist: 'Explore sub-destinations' section with links
   - If leaf (CITY): show existing destination page content (accommodations, etc.)

**Consider:** Different templates for different destination types (country overview vs city detail)

**Test requirements:**
- Manual test: Navigate to /destinos/argentina/litoral/entre-rios/depto-uruguay/concepcion-del-uruguay
- Verify breadcrumb renders correctly
- Verify children section for non-leaf destinations
- Verify 404 for invalid paths

**Acceptance criteria (US-003, US-004):**
- Hierarchical URLs resolve correctly
- Breadcrumb displays full path
- Non-leaf destinations show children navigation
- Leaf destinations show full destination content

subtasks.title: Fetch destination by path from API
Fetch breadcrumb and children from API
Render children navigation for non-leaf destinations

tasks.title: End-to-end validation and quality assurance
tasks.description:
Comprehensive end-to-end validation of the entire hierarchy system.

**Validation checklist:**
1. Database:
   - All hierarchy columns populated correctly
   - Indexes exist and are used (EXPLAIN ANALYZE)
2. Seeds:
   - `pnpm db:fresh` creates all hierarchy data
   - 15 destinations (4 hierarchy + 11 cities)
3. API:
   - All 5 hierarchy endpoints return correct data
   - Search filters work correctly
   - Performance: all queries < 100ms
4. Frontend:
   - Hierarchical URLs resolve correctly
   - Breadcrumb renders on all destination pages
   - Children navigation works for non-leaf
   - Search filters functional
5. Regression:
   - Existing destination CRUD still works
   - Existing tests pass
   - No TypeScript errors (`pnpm typecheck`)
   - Lint passes (`pnpm lint`)

**Commands to run:**
- `pnpm typecheck`
- `pnpm lint`
- `pnpm test`
- `pnpm build`

**Acceptance criteria:**
- All checks pass
- No regressions
- Performance within spec requirements

subtasks.title: Manual smoke test of hierarchy flows
Performance spot check on hierarchy queries

tasks.description:
